back_translation: Paul, an apostle of Lord Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother
To the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Lord Christ who are in the city of Colossae: may grace and peace from God our Father abide with you
We give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, always when we pray for you
Because we have heard about your faith in Lord Jesus Christ, and the love which you have for all the saints
Because of the hope that has been stored up for you in heaven, of which you have heard already in the word of truth — that is, the gospel
Which has come to you; throughout the world, the gospel is bearing fruit and growing, just as it has happened among you from the day you heard and truly knew the grace of God in truth
Just as you learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow-slave, who is a faithful servant of Lord Christ on our behalf
Who also informed us of your love in the Spirit

For this reason, from the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the true knowledge of His will, with all wisdom and spiritual understanding
So that you may walk in life worthy of Lord Jesus, being pleasing to Him in every way — bearing fruit in every good work, and growing in the true knowledge of God
Strengthened with all power according to the glorious might of His, so that you may have all endurance and patience, with joy
Giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified you to share in the inheritance of the saints in the light
He has rescued us from the authority of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of His beloved Son
In the Son, we receive redemption — that is, the forgiveness of sins
The Son is the image of God, who is unable to be seen by humans; He is the firstborn over all that He created

Because in Him, all things were created — both in heaven and on earth, both things visible and invisible — whether thrones, or ruling-deities, or powerful angels, or those-having-authority; all things were created through Him and for Him
He existed before all things, and in Him, all things continue to exist together
He is the head of the body — that is, the church; He is the beginning and is the firstborn from among the dead, so that He Himself may be first in all things
Because God was pleased to have all the fullness dwell in Him

And was pleased to reconcile all things to Himself through the Son, by making peace through the blood that was shed on His cross — whether things on earth or things in heaven, all things reconciled through Him
And you yourselves once were separated from God, and were enemies in your minds through evil deeds
But now He has reconciled you by His physical body of flesh through death, in order to bring you to appear before His presence as holy, blameless, and free from accusation

If indeed you continue in faith firmly and steadfastly, and are not shaken from the hope of the gospel which you heard, which has been proclaimed to every creature created under heaven, and I, Paul, have become a servant of that gospel
Now, I rejoice in the suffering I endure for you, and I fill up in my flesh what is still lacking in the sufferings of Lord Christ, for the sake of His body — that is, the church
I have become a servant of the church, according to the mission that God bestowed upon me for your sake, so that I would proclaim the word of God in completeness
That is, the mystery hidden throughout the ages and throughout the generations, but now has been revealed to His saints
Whom God was pleased to make known what the glorious riches of this mystery are among the Gentiles, which is — Lord Christ in you, the hope of glory
We proclaim Him by admonishing every person and teaching every person with all wisdom, so that we may bring every person to appear as one who is complete in Lord Christ
For this purpose, I labor and struggle, according to the powerful working of His that works within me with might
